stack_close.reason: reduced literal hitmax but converted failures into late EOS, repeated joints, extreme overgeneration, and worse coordinates

static_cross_attention_residual.reason: learned nearly uniform attention and a token-shared bias instead of anchor-specific motion

anchor_motion_residual.reason: the 5000-exposure probe stayed equivalent to the untrained trackable-static path: all-row F1 0.595 versus 0.605 and J2J 0.03646 versus 0.03634; normal, zero-motion, and static-bypass generations were nearly identical because the zero-initialized output layer starved the motion encoder and the fuser could use a static-only shortcut

query_rigid_motion.reason: matched154 inference was neutral (legal 107 versus 106, all-row F1 0.413997 versus 0.413442, rescue 7 versus regress 8), and residual rigid-motion ratios did not distinguish historical failures from matched controls

verified_fixed_causes: the formal rootless-v3 data contract contains no synthetic root or generated tail tokens
query mesh and target skeleton use the same selected pose and query-bbox coordinate system
flat parent-coordinate tokens can lose parent identity after coordinate quantization
stack-close restores exact parent identity but does not fix early coordinate grounding
oracle action and parent rollout fixes EOS/count but leaves hard-row geometry unusable
surface FPS randomness triggers some failures but is not the systematic cause
verified_open_facts: the current failure starts with condition-to-root or condition-to-first-child coordinate error and then amplifies through self-prefix rollout
motion is not ignored: zeroing motion worsens hard-row teacher-forcing CE, but the learned response is not reliably calibrated
zero-motion evidence changes the dynamic condition about 7.3 times more than the normal-versus-zero motion signal, so the random motion encoder rewrites static geometry strongly
the Fourier surface tokenizer already contributes most of the nonlinear response to small motion
hard diagnostic rows contain more whole-object root trajectory and less internal articulation than matched controls
the production loader currently performs evidence FPS on raw vertex displacement, so global SE(3) motion can dominate frame selection
fixed condition scaling and input-only reliability gates failed to improve matched free generation

post-loader rigid alignment on selected18 looked beneficial, but the full matched154 intervention was neutral: normal versus rigid legal 107 versus 106, all-row F1 0.413997 versus 0.413442, rescue 7 versus regress 8
on the historical-failure half of matched154, rigid alignment rescued 5 and regressed 6; it is not an inference-time repair
query-rigid preprocessing removes a real nuisance variable but is not failure-specific and does not earn the single formal training opportunity
the anchor-residual 5k checkpoint retained a 0.25007 gate but its final update weight std was only 0.00049; motion affected the fused condition by only 0.000288 of static RMS
the anchor-residual fuser was not motion-identifiable because it consumed both static and dynamic tokens; about 79.5 percent of its small residual energy was token-shared

on hard6, normal motion improved first-child and later-coordinate NLL slightly but worsened root coordinate bins (12.07 versus 6.07 under zero motion), so motion must not be treated as a uniformly beneficial global condition
unknowns: whether replacing the motion encoder's zero-motion rewrite with query-pose static tokens while retaining only normal-minus-zero motion response improves hard free generation
whether the useful motion response should enter only after root grounding rather than through one global condition for every autoregressive step
whether any identity-preserving condition route can improve failures without degrading matched legal controls
next_required_result: Run the no-weight-change centered-motion intervention from commit 0e015cca8d90a83391bbc431d48381a02b3ea357 on the fixed selected18 set: compare the accepted flat 80k dynamic condition, query-pose static condition, and query_static + alpha * (dynamic_normal - dynamic_query_repeated). Only expand the best alpha to matched154 if it improves hard legality/root/F1 without degrading controls. Do not train or submit the formal two-A100 task from the current state.

active_operation.name: centered_motion_condition_intervention_20260723
active_operation.dev_resources: Westlake reverse proxy port 20002 closes before the SSH banner and EasyConnect is not authenticated; HGC qlogin 124763552 is occupied by unrelated R009 work and must not be interrupted
active_operation.formal_resources: two A100-80GB in an allowed Westlake resource group
active_operation.acceptance_test: fixed-row free-generation legality/hitmax, all-row topology F1, J2J, count error, root and first-child error, exact query/pose/FPS contract, and visual overlays; improvement on failures must not materially regress controls

last_operation.name: anchor_motion_residual_parameter_and_condition_audit_20260723
last_operation.conclusion: the 5k route was effectively the untrained static UniRig path rather than a learned motion residual; extending it or combining it with query-rigid would not be an evidence-based use of GPU time
